excerpt: the minarets from which it is called exemplify the Prophet's station as the highest and noblest of the physical limits
source_authority: foundational Fatimid jurisprudence and esoteric exegesis

excerpt: The adhān comprises twenty-two words, corresponding to the twenty-two words of the Qur'an—understood in Ismaili exegesis as the succession of Imams from ʿAlī ibn Abī Ṭālib to Imam ʿAlī al-Ṭayyib
source_location_hint: treatise on the sources of gnosis and Imamate

excerpt: These four magnifications are grounded in the four supreme limits of religion—the First Intellect, the Second Intellect, Intellect proper, and Foundation
source_location_hint: exposition of the ten limits (hudūd) of religion

excerpt: The first attestation honors Muhammad Muṣṭafā; the second honors Maulana Muhammad ibn Ismāʿīl, the Seventh Imam, through whom the concealed Imam era commenced
source_authority: Fatimid Ismaili doctrine of the Imamate
source_location_hint: section on the continuation of guidance after the Prophet

excerpt: At a higher mystical level, it alludes to a moment when the veils between Muhammad and ʿAlī were momentarily equalized in the Triple Crown
source_location_hint: spiritual hierarchy and stations of the Prophets and Imams

excerpt: The Iqāma comprises nineteen words, each corresponding to one of the Imams—from al-Ḥusayn ibn ʿAlī through Imam ʿAlī al-Ṭayyib—excluding the two concealed Imams
source_location_hint: esoteric interpretation of the standing prayer and its ritual components

excerpt: This single utterance reaffirms that the last limit—the Qāʾim al-Qiyāma—stands alone in the rank of monotheistic uniqueness, their station so exalted that none shall ever equal them in the physical realm
source_location_hint: doctrine of the final Imam and the seal of prophecy

excerpt: When he proclaims 'Hayya ʿala al-falāḥ,' the forms of faith radiate forth—the supreme radiance—and thus he declares 'Mashā Allah'
source_authority: Fatimid Ismaili metaphysics of emanation and radiance
source_location_hint: on the illumination of spiritual forms and divine manifestation

excerpt: the Prophet conveyed to al-Bāqir, through Jābir, instructions for his training within the Assembly of the Small (Majmaʿ al-Ṣaghīr), that hidden convocation where esoteric knowledge is transmitted
source_location_hint: sessions recording the transmission of prophetic knowledge to the Imams
